title: Build PSP (Podman Socket Proxy): policy-gated Testcontainers runtime for sandboxed agents
description:
Objective
Create PSP as a standalone project that enables Testcontainers usage from sandboxed agents without exposing raw host Podman socket access.

Scope
- Docker-compatible broker API fronting rootless Podman
- Policy enforcement and deny-by-default posture
- Session isolation, cleanup, and auditability
- Clear integration contract for AGS

Success Criteria
- Agents can run integration tests through PSP endpoint
- Dangerous container operations are blocked by policy
- Lifecycle/cleanup is deterministic
- AGS integration requires only minimal wiring

Out of Scope
- Multi-tenant auth system
- Managed cloud service deployment
- Non-Linux runtime parity beyond explicit docs

title: Write ADR + threat model for PSP trust boundaries
description:
Goal
Lock architecture and threat model before implementation.

Deliverables
- ADR describing boundaries: sandbox process, PSP, host Podman API
- Abuse case matrix and mitigations
- Rationale for broker vs raw socket mounting

Acceptance Criteria
- ADR approved and actionable for implementation tasks
- Residual risks explicitly documented

title: Implement PSP MVP Docker-compatible API broker
description:
Goal
Implement core Docker-compatible API broker to satisfy Testcontainers base lifecycle operations.

Scope
- Unix socket listener
- Forward/translate required endpoints to rootless Podman API
- Consistent error mapping for client compatibility

Acceptance Criteria
- End-to-end create/start/inspect/logs/wait/remove works through PSP
- Unsupported endpoints fail with explicit structured errors

title: Add PSP policy engine (deny by default)
description:
Goal
Prevent privilege escalation and unsafe runtime operations.

Policy v1
- deny privileged mode and host namespaces
- deny unrestricted bind mounts (allowlist only)
- deny device mounts and dangerous capability adds
- optional image allow/deny controls

Acceptance Criteria
- Policies are schema-validated and version controlled
- Denials include stable rule IDs and human-readable reasons
- Unit tests cover allow/deny matrix
